figures.caption: Fig. 2. SSP-RK3 convergence validation. (a) Linear advection. (b) 1D inviscid Burgers (nonlinear) via MMS. (c) 2D advection via dimensional splitting.
Fig. 3. Classical RK4 convergence validation. (a) Linear advection. (b) 1D Burgers (MMS). (c) 2D advection.
Table 4 Comparison of stability and convergence traits across both integrators.
Fig. 9. SSP-RK3: Modified wavenumber (dispersion) and numerical dissipation analysis of the boundary closures.
Table 2 Classical RK4 boundary closure weights: standard Taylor closures vs. accuracy-driven and stability-augmented optimisations.

Fig. 8. Classical RK4: Eigenvalue spectra overlaid on the RK4 stability lobe at CFL = 0.5. (a) Standard vs. accuracy-only. (b) Standard vs. stability-augmented. The accuracy-only stencils remain stable up to CFL ≈ 1.25, while the stability-augmented variant extends the range beyond CFL ≈ 1.5.
Fig. 7. SSP-RK3: Eigenvalue spectra overlaid on the stability lobe. (a) Standard vs. accuracyonly. (b) Standard vs. stability-augmented.
Table 5 Feature comparison of the three main approaches to order-reduction mitigation.

Fig. 13. Convergence on the coupled 1D linearised Euler equations. (a) SSP-RK3 with order3 WSO methods. (b) Classical RK4 with the order-4 (6,4,3) WSO method. Optimised boundary stencils restore high-order convergence while WSO methods show no improvement.
Fig. 5. SSP-RK3 with standard and optimised stencils vs. three order-3 WSO methods with standard stencils: (a) 1D Linear Advection, (b) 1D Burgers (MMS), (c) 2D Linear Advection.
Fig. 6. Classical RK4 with standard and optimised stencils vs. the order-4 WSO method Biswas (6,4,3) with standard stencils: (a) 1D Linear Advection, (b) 1D Burgers (MMS), (c) 2D Linear Advection.

Fig. 12. Trajectory of the most dominant eigenvalue pair for the stability-augmented stencils across increasing CFL numbers {0.2, 0.4, . . . , 1.2}, for both SSP-RK3 and classical RK4. The background contour represents the distance from the origin within the stability envelope.
Fig. 11. Gershgorin discs for the SSP-RK3 boundary closure rows (solid: row 1, dashed: row 2) overlaid on the stability region at CFL = 0.5. (a) Standard. (b) Accuracy-only. (c) Acc.+stab.

Fig. 4. Measured convergence order versus CFL number. (a) SSP-RK3. (b) Classical RK4. The accuracy-only stencils collapse at their respective CFL limits, while the stability-augmented variants maintain convergence across wider ranges.
Fig. 10. Classical RK4: Modified wavenumber (dispersion) and numerical dissipation analysis of the optimised boundary stencils.
